techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries may use valid accounts to gain initial access, persist, or escalate privileges. Overly permissive IAM policies and wildcard permissions enable abuse of legitimate credentials.
techniques.cloud_examples: IAM policies with Action:*, wildcard Resource, broad assume-role trust policies, default service accounts with admin access
techniques.detection_patterns: iam_policy, iam_role, assume_role, Action, *, AdministratorAccess, principal, aws_iam

techniques.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries may use cloud accounts with excessive permissions. Shared credentials, long-lived access keys, and missing MFA enable unauthorized access.
techniques.cloud_examples: IAM users without MFA, long-lived access keys, shared service account keys, root account usage
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_iam_user, access_key, mfa, login_profile, google_service_account_key, azurerm_user

techniques.name: Data from Cloud Storage
techniques.description: Adversaries may access data from improperly secured cloud storage. Public buckets, missing encryption, and overly broad access policies expose sensitive data.
techniques.cloud_examples: Public S3 buckets, public-read ACL, missing server-side encryption, open bucket policies, public Azure blob containers
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_s3_bucket, acl, public-read, public, bucket_policy, encryption, server_side_encryption, azurerm_storage_account, google_storage_bucket

techniques.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
techniques.description: Adversaries may transfer data to another cloud account they control. Cross-account access without restrictions and overly permissive sharing policies enable exfiltration.
techniques.cloud_examples: S3 bucket policies allowing cross-account access, unrestricted AssumeRole from external accounts, shared snapshots
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_s3_bucket_policy, cross_account, external_id, sts, assume_role, snapshot, ami, shared

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Adversaries exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing services. Open security groups, public endpoints without authentication, and exposed management interfaces enable initial access.
techniques.cloud_examples: Security groups with 0.0.0.0/0 ingress, public subnets without NACLs, exposed SSH/RDP ports, public load balancers without WAF
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_security_group, ingress, 0.0.0.0/0, cidr_blocks, ::/0, from_port, to_port, publicly_accessible, azurerm_network_security_rule

techniques.name: Account Manipulation
techniques.description: Adversaries may manipulate accounts to maintain access or escalate privileges. Unrestricted role creation, inline admin policies, and overly broad permission boundaries enable persistence.
techniques.cloud_examples: IAM policies allowing iam:*, unrestricted CreateRole/AttachPolicy, missing permission boundaries, inline admin policies
techniques.detection_patterns: iam_policy, iam:*, CreateRole, AttachRolePolicy, PutRolePolicy, permissions_boundary, inline_policy

techniques.name: Impair Defenses
techniques.description: Adversaries may disable or tamper with security monitoring. Disabled logging, removed CloudTrail, turned off GuardDuty, or suppressed alerts allow adversaries to operate undetected.
techniques.cloud_examples: CloudTrail with logging disabled, S3 bucket logging off, GuardDuty disabled, VPC flow logs missing, disabled Azure Security Center
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_cloudtrail, is_multi_region_trail, enable_logging, aws_guardduty, vpc_flow_log, logging, monitoring, azurerm_security_center

techniques.name: Impair Defenses: Disable Cloud Logs
techniques.description: Adversaries may disable cloud logging to avoid detection. Turning off CloudTrail, deleting log groups, or disabling access logging removes audit trails.
techniques.cloud_examples: CloudTrail with enable_logging=false, missing S3 access logging, deleted CloudWatch log groups, disabled GCP audit logs
techniques.detection_patterns: enable_logging, false, cloudtrail, cloudwatch_log_group, access_logging, audit_log_config

techniques.name: Cloud Infrastructure Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may discover cloud infrastructure details. Exposed metadata endpoints, IMDSv1 usage, and overly broad describe/list permissions reveal infrastructure topology.
techniques.cloud_examples: EC2 instances using IMDSv1 instead of IMDSv2, overly broad ec2:Describe* permissions, exposed metadata services
techniques.detection_patterns: metadata_options, http_tokens, http_endpoint, IMDSv2, instance_metadata, ec2:Describe, http_put_response_hop_limit

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials
techniques.description: Adversaries may search for unsecured credentials in cloud infrastructure code. Hardcoded secrets, API keys, passwords, and connection strings in plaintext enable credential theft.
techniques.cloud_examples: Hardcoded AWS access keys in Terraform, passwords in user_data scripts, API keys in environment variables, plaintext secrets in config
techniques.detection_patterns: access_key, secret_key, password, api_key, connection_string, user_data, AKIA, private_key, credentials

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.description: Adversaries may access the cloud instance metadata API to collect credentials. IMDSv1 allows unauthenticated access to instance credentials via HTTP.
techniques.cloud_examples: EC2 instances without IMDSv2 enforcement, missing http_tokens=required, metadata endpoint exposed to containers
techniques.detection_patterns: metadata_options, http_tokens, optional, required, http_endpoint, instance_metadata_tags

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data using non-standard protocols. Unrestricted egress rules, open outbound ports, and missing network ACLs enable data exfiltration.
techniques.cloud_examples: Security groups with unrestricted egress (0.0.0.0/0 on all ports), missing outbound NACLs, no VPC endpoints for S3
techniques.detection_patterns: egress, outbound, 0.0.0.0/0, all, nacl, network_acl, vpc_endpoint, aws_security_group_rule

techniques.name: Resource Hijacking
techniques.description: Adversaries may hijack cloud resources for cryptomining or other purposes. Missing resource constraints, no budget alerts, and unbounded instance types enable resource abuse.
techniques.cloud_examples: No AWS budget alerts, unbounded auto-scaling groups, large instance types without justification, missing resource quotas
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_budgets_budget, autoscaling_group, max_size, instance_type, quota, budget, cost

techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Adversaries may use brute force to obtain credentials. Missing account lockout policies, weak password requirements, and exposed authentication endpoints enable brute force attacks.
techniques.cloud_examples: IAM password policy with short minimum length, no account lockout, Cognito without MFA, exposed RDS/database ports
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_iam_account_password_policy, minimum_password_length, max_password_age, require_uppercase, cognito, password_policy

techniques.name: Create Account: Cloud Account
techniques.description: Adversaries may create cloud accounts to maintain persistence. Unrestricted ability to create IAM users or service accounts enables backdoor access.
techniques.cloud_examples: IAM policies allowing iam:CreateUser without conditions, service account creation without org policy constraints
techniques.detection_patterns: iam:CreateUser, iam:CreateAccessKey, CreateServiceAccount, create_user, aws_iam_user

techniques.name: Implant Internal Image
techniques.description: Adversaries may implant backdoors in cloud images. Public AMIs, unverified container images, and registries without scanning enable supply chain attacks.
techniques.cloud_examples: Public AMIs used without verification, ECR without image scanning, Docker images from untrusted registries
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_ami, image_id, ecr, image_scanning_configuration, scan_on_push, container_registry, docker

techniques.name: Modify Authentication Process
techniques.description: Adversaries may modify authentication mechanisms. Custom identity providers, modified federation settings, or weakened auth requirements enable unauthorized access.
techniques.cloud_examples: SAML/OIDC providers with weak validation, Cognito with no MFA, modified IAM identity providers, weakened conditional access
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_iam_saml_provider, aws_iam_openid_connect_provider, cognito_user_pool, mfa_configuration, identity_provider

techniques.name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure
techniques.description: Adversaries may modify cloud compute infrastructure to evade detection. Creating snapshots, modifying instances, or changing security groups can bypass controls.
techniques.cloud_examples: Unrestricted EC2 modify/run permissions, ability to create snapshots of any volume, unrestricted security group modification
techniques.detection_patterns: ec2:ModifyInstanceAttribute, ec2:RunInstances, ec2:CreateSnapshot, modify_instance, create_snapshot

techniques.name: Deploy Container
techniques.description: Adversaries may deploy containers to execute malicious code. Privileged containers, host network access, and missing pod security policies enable container breakout.
techniques.cloud_examples: Privileged containers in ECS/EKS, host network mode, missing pod security standards, containers running as root
techniques.detection_patterns: privileged, host_network, security_context, run_as_root, pod_security, ecs_task_definition, eks, container_definitions

techniques.name: Escape to Host
techniques.description: Adversaries may escape container isolation to access the host. Privileged mode, host path mounts, and capability additions enable container escape.
techniques.cloud_examples: Docker containers with --privileged, host volume mounts, added Linux capabilities (SYS_ADMIN), missing seccomp profiles
techniques.detection_patterns: privileged, host_path, volume_mount, capabilities, SYS_ADMIN, seccomp, AppArmor, security_context

techniques.name: Container and Resource Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may discover containers and cloud resources. Overly broad list/describe permissions on ECS, EKS, or other container services reveal infrastructure.
techniques.cloud_examples: Broad ecs:List*/Describe* permissions, exposed Kubernetes API server, public container registry
techniques.detection_patterns: ecs:List, ecs:Describe, eks:List, eks:Describe, kubernetes, api_server, container_registry, ecr

techniques.name: Cloud Storage Object Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may enumerate cloud storage objects. Broad s3:List* permissions and bucket policies without conditions enable storage enumeration.
techniques.cloud_examples: IAM policies with s3:ListBucket on *, public bucket listing enabled, storage account with public blob enumeration
techniques.detection_patterns: s3:ListBucket, s3:GetObject, list_objects, public_access, blob_public_access, storage_account

techniques.name: Data Destruction
techniques.description: Adversaries may destroy cloud data. Missing deletion protection, no versioning on S3 buckets, and unrestricted delete permissions enable permanent data loss.
techniques.cloud_examples: S3 buckets without versioning, RDS without deletion protection, DynamoDB without PITR, missing backup policies
techniques.detection_patterns: versioning, deletion_protection, backup, point_in_time_recovery, lifecycle, mfa_delete, prevent_destroy

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
techniques.description: Adversaries may encrypt data for ransom. Unrestricted KMS key usage, missing key rotation, and broad encryption/decryption permissions enable ransomware attacks.
techniques.cloud_examples: KMS keys without rotation, broad kms:Encrypt/Decrypt permissions, customer-managed keys accessible to external accounts
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_kms_key, enable_key_rotation, kms:Encrypt, kms:Decrypt, key_policy, key_rotation

techniques.name: Endpoint Denial of Service
techniques.description: Adversaries may perform denial of service attacks on cloud endpoints. Missing rate limiting, no WAF, and exposed APIs without throttling enable service disruption.
techniques.cloud_examples: API Gateway without throttling, CloudFront without WAF, ALB without rate limiting, missing DDoS protection (Shield)
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_api_gateway, throttling, waf, aws_wafv2, shield, rate_limit, cloudfront, api_gateway_method_settings

techniques.name: Remote Services: SSH
techniques.description: Adversaries may use SSH for lateral movement. Open SSH ports to the internet, key-based auth without restrictions, and missing bastion hosts enable unauthorized remote access.
techniques.cloud_examples: Security groups with port 22 open to 0.0.0.0/0, EC2 instances with public IPs and open SSH, missing bastion/jump hosts
techniques.detection_patterns: port, 22, ssh, 0.0.0.0/0, bastion, key_name, associate_public_ip_address

techniques.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
techniques.description: Adversaries may use stolen application access tokens. Long-lived tokens, missing token rotation, and broadly scoped OAuth grants enable unauthorized API access.
techniques.cloud_examples: Lambda functions with broad execution roles, API keys without expiration, OAuth scopes without least privilege
techniques.detection_patterns: lambda, execution_role, api_key, token, oauth, scope, expiration, rotation

techniques.name: Cloud Service Dashboard
techniques.description: Adversaries may use cloud dashboards to discover resources. Console access without IP restrictions and missing session policies enable reconnaissance via the management console.
techniques.cloud_examples: IAM users with console access but no IP restriction, missing session duration limits, no conditional access policies
techniques.detection_patterns: console_access, login_profile, session_duration, ip_restriction, condition, source_ip, aws:SourceIp

techniques.name: Acquire Infrastructure: Web Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may use web services for C2 or staging. Unrestricted Lambda/Cloud Function invocation and public API endpoints enable adversary infrastructure within the victim's cloud.
techniques.cloud_examples: Publicly invocable Lambda functions, API Gateway without auth, Cloud Functions with allUsers invoke permission
techniques.detection_patterns: aws_lambda_permission, principal, *, invoke, api_gateway_authorizer, allUsers, function_url

techniques.name: Service Stop
techniques.description: Adversaries may stop cloud services to cause disruption. Unrestricted stop/terminate permissions and missing termination protection enable service disruption.
techniques.cloud_examples: EC2 without termination protection, RDS without deletion protection, unrestricted ec2:StopInstances/TerminateInstances
techniques.detection_patterns: disable_api_termination, termination_protection, ec2:StopInstances, ec2:TerminateInstances, deletion_protection
